lines.text: The Wedding Hall, Monica and Chandler have just said "I do," and the photographer is taking the required pictures. First of Monica, Chandler, Ross and Joey.

lines.speaker: Photographer
lines.text: Great. (Takes a picture.) Great! Just give me a sec to change film.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Monica) Okay, I know I'm not supposed to know, but I do. And I'm so excited for you!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? What's going on?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Monica's pregnant!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh my God! Is that why you guys had to get married?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Guys! I'm not pregnant.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah. (To Chandler) Slow swimmers? (Chandler looks at him.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?! What do you mean? You-you-you're not pregnant?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You didn't tell anybody I was did you?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! (Pause) I'll be right back. (Exits.)

lines.speaker: Photographer
lines.text: Now why don't we get a shot of just Monica and the bloody soldier.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (To Joey) Oh, about that. Joey, you have to change before the party.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can't! I-I don't have any other clothes here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Find some! Please! Anything that doesn't say I-I died tragically in France. (Joey leaves to find clothes.)

lines.speaker: Photographer
lines.text: Well then why don't we see the bride and the groom and the bridemaids.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (Phoebe and Rachel join Monica and Chandler on the altar.) Hey Mon, why did you tell the guys you weren't pregnant?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because I'm not.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We found your test in the trash, if you're not pregnant-(She sees Rachel shaking her head)-It's because I am.
Flash, the photographer takes a picture of Monica and Chandler's stunned faces.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?! What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, I...I am with child. (Flash) And I didn't want to say anything because it's your day; I didn't want to steal your thunder.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait a minute! So you told people I was pregnant?! (Flash) Does this look like a conversation that I want to remember?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Who's the father?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can't say.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can't say because he's famous.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God, who is it?! (Phoebe rolls her eyes.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, come on, you have to tell us.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, okay. It's James Brolin. James Brolin is the father of my baby.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: As in Barbara Streisand's husband James Brolin?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! Well he never said that to me!
Opening Credits
The Wedding Hall, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Photographer
lines.text: Why don't we have Monica step away and we'll get Chandler and the bridemaids.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How about just the bridemaids?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know I am the groom right? I was told it was kinda big deal.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It is.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: For you. (Chandler leaves.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, thank you for doing that. I just can't deal with this just quite yet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So instead you told me Monica was pregnant.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You said that she was, I just didn't disagree with you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sneaky.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah.

lines.speaker: Photographer
lines.text: Smile ladies.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! (They smile and the picture is taken.) Oh by the way?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-hmm.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: James Brolin?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I know. I could only think of two names, him and Ed Begley Jr. and then I remembered he's gay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ed Begley Jr. is not gay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (intrigued) Really?!
The Reception Hall, the party is in full swing.

lines.speaker: Bandleader
lines.text: Thank you very much! Ladies and gentlemen, it gives me great pleasure to introduce to you for the very first time, Mr. and Mrs. Chandler Bing!
They enter.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Before we go out there I've got a present for ya.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, I'm going to put my hand in your pocket!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I've been taking dancing lessons.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, the last six weeks. I wanted this to be a moment you will never forget.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh that is so sweet!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So? Would you care to join me in our first dance as husband and wife?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes.
They walk onto the dance floor and Chandler slips and almost falls.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What's the matter?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know, it's these new shoes, they're all slippery.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, are you going to be able to do this?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Not well.
They start dancing and Chandler starts slipping around.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, the good news is, I don't think anyone's looking at us.
[Cut to Phoebe and Rachel at their table.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, are you ready to talk about it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.
Pause

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Now?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, we'll talk about something else then.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Who's the father?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh! Look honey y'know what? I haven't told him yet, so until I do I don't think I should tell anybody else.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. That's fine. That's fair. Is it Tag?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I'm sorry. I'll stop.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Is it Ross? It's Ross isn't it-Oh my God, it's Joey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, stop it! I am not going to tell you until I tell him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ah-hah! At least we know it's a him.
[Cut to Joey entering wearing a preppy tennis outfit.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (seeing him) Oh sweet Lord.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm sorry! Okay? I went down to the gift shop and it's either this or a bathrobe! Look, what's more important, the way I'm dressed or me being with you on your special day?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, I'm not even going to pretend I was listening. (Sees someone else.) Hey! Hey! (Goes over to that person.)
[Cut to the hallway outside the room, Ross is going to see which table he's at and sees a beautiful woman doing the same thing.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi. I'm uh, I'm Ross. I don't, I don't believe we've met. I'm Monica's older brother.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Oh hi, I'm, I'm Mona from her restaurant.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! Hello uh, Mona from her restaurant. (He uses his card to mouth those words.) (Pause) Mona, wow what a, what a beautiful name.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: You think so? I've always kinda hated it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Aw come on, Mona Lisa?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mona umm...Clickclocken. The famous botanist? Huh? Oh no she's uh-well she's dead now. No, supposedly she was once quite the hottie of the plant world.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Really?! Well see? I never knew about her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Linda Clickclocken. (Pause) So what uh, what-what table are you at? (She shows him.) Oh, uh me too.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Oh good. Now there'll be someone there who likes my name.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (sexily) Yes there will. (Mona leaves and Ross tries to find the same table.) Oh guess what, Molly Gilbert you've just been bumped up to table one. And if it's all right with you I'm gonna take your place at table six-Martin Clickclocken.
[Cut back inside to Joey giving a beautiful woman a tennis lesson by standing behind her.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's better, now just bend your arms a little more. There you go. Okay, look straight ahead. Now this time I want you to really put your ass into it. (They do a practice swing and she really puts her ass into it.)
[Cut to Chandler sitting down near Joey as his mom walks over. His birth mother, not the mother who recently visited one of those clinics in Sweden.]

lines.speaker: Mrs. Bing
lines.text: Chandler darling! Look, my date has finally arrived. I'd like you to meet Dennis Phillips.

lines.speaker: Dennis Phillips
lines.text: Congratulations.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Bing
lines.text: Dennis is a dear old friend and a fantastic lover.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bravo Dennis thanks for pleasing my mother so.

lines.speaker: Dennis Phillips
lines.text: Oh, I'm so sorry I missed the ceremony, I was stuck at auditions.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Bing
lines.text: Oh yes, Dennis is directing a new Broadway show.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't believe we've met, Joey Tribbiani.

lines.speaker: Dennis Phillips
lines.text: Dennis Phillips

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow, I've admired your work for years. You-you've done some really amazing stuff.

lines.speaker: Dennis Phillips
lines.text: Oh, thank you. Well if you'll excuse me, I'm gonna go get myself a drink. Be back in a moment. (Walks away.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow Dennis Phillips! That's great! How did you guys meet?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Bing
lines.text: Well, it's a funny story.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Funny: ha-ha or funny: (Mimes blowing his brain out.)
[Time lapse. The band is finishing another song.]

lines.speaker: Bandleader
lines.text: Thank you, thank you very much. If everyone will please take your seats, dinner will be served.
Ross starts looking for table six and finds out that it's the kid's table. He sees Mona sitting at another table.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! Uh, I thought, I thought you were at table six.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: No, nine. (Shows him the card again.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh see, before you uh, when you showed it to me you-you held it that way (he turns her hand upside down) which uh, which was misleading. Well I'm... (He goes at sits down at his new table and the kids stare at him.) Hello.
[Cut to the hallway, Chandler is putting tape on the bottom of his shoes.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler. Will you see if your mom can give my resume to Dennis Phillips? 'Cause if I can get in a Broadway show then I would've done it all, film, television, and theater. The only think left would be radio, and that's just for ugly people.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What size shoes do you wear?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, eleven, eleven and a half.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Great, because my shoes are giving me a little problem on the dance floor, can I borrow the boots from your costume?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, I don't even really know where I left those. Sorry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (looking at Joey's feet) Those aren't eleven and a half.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay fine! I'm a seven! All right, I have surprisingly small feet. But the rest of me is good, I'll show ya!
[Cut to Monica and Rachel at their table.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Can you believe Phoebe got pregnant?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh y'know what honey? Let's not talk about that right now?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is so huge.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sure, but come on, as big as your wedding?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Of course not nothing is. Between me and you...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: ...in this day and age how dumb do you have to be to get pregnant?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! Y'know, sometimes you can do everything right, everyone can wear everything they're supposed to wear, and one of those little guys just gets through!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know! Maybe they have tools.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well I-I talked to and uh, she's definitely going to have this baby. Y'know, she said she was gonna raise it on her own.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, maybe that's, maybe that's really brave.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Maybe. I just hope she realizes how hard it's gonna be.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Maybe she hasn't really thought it through that well.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, there's a lot to think about. I mean, how is she, how is she going to handle this financially? How is she going to juggle work? Does she realize she's not going to have a date again for the next eighteen years?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (starting to cry) I don't know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-hmm. I'm just thinking about Phoebe; poor knocked up Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Champagne?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yes! Thank you very much! (She grabs a glass, takes a sip, and realizes what she just did. She then tries to spit the champagne back into the glass without Monica noticing. It doesn't work.) Oh that's-that's actually how the French drink it.
Monica gasps.
Commercial Break
continued from earlier, only now Phoebe joins them.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I just got off the phone with my lover, James Brolin...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh really?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, and apparently he is married to some singer, but he said he would leave her for me. And I said, "James, James Brolin, are you sure?" James Brolin said...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (interrupting) Rachel's really the one who's pregnant.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (shocked) What?! (deadpan) Why bother?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How do you feel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know. I don't know how I feel. This is all happening so fast. I have to make all these decisions that I don't want to make. (Takes another sip of champagne and spits it back out) Somebody just take this away from me!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Calm down. Maybe you're not pregnant.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: When I got pregnant with the triplets, I took that test like three times just to make sure.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes! Maybe it's a false positive. Are you sure you peed on the stick right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How many ways are there to do that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm-I'm just saying, don't freak out until you're a hundred percent sure.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, I'll-I'll take it again when I get home.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You-you gotta take it now. Come on, do it as a present to me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'll run out and get you one.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you guys are so great.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, wait a minute! Who's is the father?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no, she won't tell us.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, come on it's my wedding! That can be my present.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wh-Hey, I just gave you peeing on a stick.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: See? This is why you register.
[Cut to Ross at the kiddie table. He reaches for something and a fart noise emanates which causes the kids to laugh.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It was the chair again! Okay? I'm not doing it! It what-look, I don't-y'know what-eh-eh... (He walks away and goes over to Mona.) Hi.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm, would you like to dance?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh great! (They get up to dance and Ross is interrupted by a little girl.)

lines.speaker: Little Girl
lines.text: Dr. Geller?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I wasn't farting! (To Mona) Uh, a little game from our table. (To the little girl) Yes?

lines.speaker: Little Girl
lines.text: Dr. Geller, will you dance with me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh umm, well uh, maybe-maybe later. Right now, I'm about to dance with this lady.

lines.speaker: Little Girl
lines.text: Okay. (She drops her head in disappointment and walks away.)

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Ohhhh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, unless! Unless, uh this lady wouldn't mind letting you go first.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: I'd be happy to. (To Ross) You are very sweet.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes I-I am. In fact umm hey, why don't we try it my special way? You can dance on my feet.

lines.speaker: Little Girl
lines.text: Sure!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah? Hop on. (They start dancing and Mona sits down.) Is the pretty lady looking?

lines.speaker: Little Girl
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Keep dancing.
[Cut to Chandler in the hallway practicing dancing and is doing it very well.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And the world will never know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Did you talk to Dennis about me yet?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, I told him how talented you were. I told him all about Days Of Our Lives.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no! No! No! You don't tell a Broadway guy that! Now he just thinks I'm a soap actor.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But you're not just a soap actor. You are a soap actor with freakishly tiny feet.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!
[Joey walks back inside just as Ross's dance is finishing.]

lines.speaker: Little Girl
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no, thank you Miranda.

lines.speaker: Little Girl
lines.text: Melinda!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right. (Walks over to Mona.)

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: How cute was that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh-oh, were you, were you watching?
Another little girl walks over to him.

lines.speaker: Second Girl
lines.text: Can I go next?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? Of course you can! Hop on!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Okay, but I get to hop on after her. (Ross bites the air in response.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I am so gonna score.

lines.speaker: Second Girl
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I like your bow.
[Behind them, Joey goes up to the bandleader and interrupts the song.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (clinks his glass) I'd like to propose a toast. To Monica and Chandler, the greatest couple in the world. And my best friends. Now, my when I first found out they were getting married I was, I was a little angry. I was like, (overly angry) "Why God? Why? How can you take them away from me?!" But then I thought back over all our memories together, some happy memories. (Does a fake laugh.) And-and there was some sad memories. (Starts to break down and cry.) I'm sorry. And-and some scared memories-Whoa! (He jumps back, startled.) Eh? And then, and then I realized I'll always be their friend, their friend who can speak in many dialects and has training in stage combat and is willing to do partial nudity. (Starts to walk away, but realizes something.) Oh! To the happy couple!
[Time Lapse, the interrupted song is finishing.]

lines.speaker: Bandleader
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to the second girl) That was very nice Ashley.

lines.speaker: Ashley
lines.text: Can we do it again?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no. (Walks over to Mona again.)

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: So, is it my turn now?
A large little fat girl walks over.

lines.speaker: Fat Girl
lines.text: I'm next!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! (Recoils in horror.)

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Uh, that's okay. You can dance with her first.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, you-you sure? (She nods yes.) Okay. (To the girl) Okay. So what's uh, what's your name.

lines.speaker: Fat Girl
lines.text: Gert!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's, that's pretty. (They start to dance and Gert tries to step on Ross's feet, but he pulls them out of harm's way.) Whoa! Whoa! Whoa! What are you doing there Gert?

lines.speaker: Gert
lines.text: Dancing on your feet! Like the other girls did it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. (Swallows hard.) Hop on Gert. (She does and Ross winces in pain.)

lines.speaker: Gert
lines.text: Why aren't you moving your feet?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm trying. (He strains to move his feet.)

lines.speaker: Gert
lines.text: Faster! You're not going fast enough!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Maybe I should stand on your feet! (Gert's shocked and Ross realizes what he said and tries to brush it off.)
[Cut to Joey going over to talk to Dennis Phillips.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So did you uh, happen to catch my toast up there?

lines.speaker: Dennis Phillips
lines.text: Oh my God, that was for my benefit?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I'd like to think there was something for everyone. Look, I know you're casting for this new show...

lines.speaker: Dennis Phillips
lines.text: Look umm Joey, I-I don't think you're quite right for this project.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, see that's where you're wrong. Whatever it is I can do it. And if didn't see it up there, just-just try me.

lines.speaker: Dennis Phillips
lines.text: It's an all Chinese cast. Can you be Chinese?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well I'm not proud of this, but... (He turns around and starts to mess with his eyelids.)

lines.speaker: Dennis Phillips
lines.text: Oh my God! No-no-no! Please! Please! Don't-don't-don't!
[Cut to Monica walking up to Chandler.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, are you ready to get back on the dance floor?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did it turn into sand?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh come on, I love this song! Come on, you'll be fine. (She starts to walk towards the floor.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (sliding up behind her) No. No, I won't. Do you know why I took all those lessons? See, for the first time I didn't want you to be embarrassed to be seen on the dance floor with some clumsy idiot.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh sweetie, you can never embarrass me. (Chandler grunts.) Okay, you can easily embarrass me. But come on, it doesn't matter. All right? I married you! So I want to dance on my wedding night with my husband. Come on. (They go onto the floor.) Just try not to move your feet at all. (Chandler starts to get into the groove and bust a move.) There you go.
Mr. Geller dances over.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Chandler, I'm gonna have you arrested.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: You stole my moves. (He starts to dance like Chandler was and Chandler stops.)
The Women's Restroom, Rachel and Phoebe are waiting for the outcome of Rachel's second test.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How much longer?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: 30 seconds.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: 30 seconds, okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Did I miss it? (Phoebe nods no.) Rachel, I-I want you to know that, if it's positive, we're gonna...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh I know. I know. (They hug.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's time.
Another woman starts to enter.

lines.speaker: The Girls
lines.text: No!! (The woman backs out.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Go ahead Rach.
She goes over to look.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wait! Y'know what? I can't, I can't look at it. I can't. Somebody else tell me, somebody tell me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, it's negative.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's negative.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. Oh. Well there you go. Whew! (Pause) That is-that's great-that is really great-great news. (Pause) Y'know 'cause the whole not being ready and kinda the financial aspects, all that. Whew. Wow, this is so just the way it was supposed to be. (Starts to cry.) God.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well... Well, great.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Here. (Gives Rachel a tissue.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thanks. (Crying) God this is so stupid! (Pause) How could I be upset over something I never had? It's negative?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, it's positive.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's-it's not negative, it's positive.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well yeah, I lied before.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Now you know how you really feel about it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh-oh, that's a risky little game!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you really gonna do this?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. I'm gonna have a baby. I'm gonna have a baby. I'm gonna have a baby! (They all hug.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: With who?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ah, it's still not the time.
Dedicated to the People of New York City
Closing Credits
The Reception, Joey is helping Ross walk after Gert got through with him and Mona is looking on concerned.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I just didn't see the fast song coming.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Shh. Shh. Don't try to talk, we'll get you up to your room, we'll soak your feet, you'll be okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, thank you.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: That is so sweet!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: No, I mean it. There are so few genuinely nice guys out there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Tell me about it, I feel like I'm holding down the fort all by myself.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: It's Joey right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait a minute! No! I'm the nice one! I'm the one who danced with the kids all night! How...How small are your feet?! (They all look down.)
End
